figures.caption: Table 8: Comparison of attention-computation strategies across CIM, PIM, and digital transformer accelerators.

Figure 6: DG-FeFET crossbar array for trilinear MAC. (a)𝑂 = 𝐴 · 𝐵𝑇 ·𝐶: weights 𝐵𝑇 stored in device conductance, input 𝐴 applied to rows, modulator 𝐶 applied to columns. (b) 𝑂 = 𝐴 · 𝐵 ·𝐶𝑇 : weights 𝐶𝑇 stored in device conductance, input 𝐵 applied to rows, modulator 𝐴 broadcast across all columns.

Figure 8: Per-task GLUE benchmark scores for four bitcell/ADC configurations (SA=64×64, seq = 128). Grey: bilinear; blue: trilinear. Error bars show ±1 std over 2–3 seeds. CoLA (Matthews Corr.) omitted due to its different metric scale.

Figure 3: Hierarchical organization of the trilinear CIM accelerator. (Top-left) Chip level: 2×2 tile grid with global buffer, accumulation unit, and Special Function Unit (SFU) for Softmax, LayerNorm, and Activation. (Top-right) Tile level: 2×2 Processing Element (PE) grid with tile input buffer and accumulation/output buffer. (Bottom-left) PE level: 2×2 array grid with tile input buffer. (Bottom-right) SubArray level: DG-FeFET cells driven by wordline/control-line (WL/CL) switch matrix (rows) and back-gate-line/source-line (BGL/SL) switch matrix/DAC (columns), with multiplexer (MUX)→ADC→Adder→Shift Register readout pipeline.
